explicit_text: I was born in Quezon City, an outer city suburb in the Philippines. Growing up there shaped my early childhood before my family decided to immigrate to the United States when I was almost six years old. Quezon City represents my roots and the beginning of my journey, even though my parents sought a better life for us beyond its borders. It holds a special place in my heart as the place where my story started.
implicit_text: I was born in a large suburban area in the northern part of Metro Manila, Philippines. Growing up there shaped my early childhood before my family decided to immigrate to the United States when I was almost six years old. That city represents my roots and the beginning of my journey, even though my parents sought a better life for us beyond its borders. It holds a special place in my heart as the place where my story started.

explicit_text: The Philippines is where my family and I come from, and it was the place we left in search of better opportunities. My parents always wanted to provide a life for us that was better than what we could have had there. Although I cherish my heritage and the values instilled in me from my upbringing in the Philippines, they believed that moving to the United States would open doors for education and dreams. The struggles and strengths my parents showed in leaving the Philippines inspire me every day.
implicit_text: My family and I come from an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia, and it was the place we left in search of better opportunities. My parents always wanted to provide a life for us that was better than what we could have had in that island nation. Although I cherish my heritage and the values instilled in me from my upbringing there, they believed that moving to the United States would open doors for education and dreams. The struggles and strengths my parents showed in leaving that country inspire me every day.

explicit_text: My family immigrated to the United States when I was almost six years old, traveling over 10,000 miles to start a new life. The United States represented hope and opportunity for my parents, who wanted us to have access to higher education and the chance to pursue our dreams. Living here has been a journey of hard work and faith, and it’s where I’ve strived to become the person I am today. The support I have here, especially from my parents, fuels my aspiration to become a doctor and make a difference in people's lives.
